name: Basic Puff Pancake
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 425°F.
2. Melt butter in an 10-inch ovenproof skillet over medium heat, tilting the pan to coat the sides.
3. Whisk milk, flour, granulated sugar, eggs, and nutmeg in a medium bowl. Pour into skillet; cook for 1 minute.
4. Place in the oven and bake until puffed and golden, 12-15 minutes. Sprinkle with confectioner's sugar or serve with topping, if desired. Serve immediately.
Variations:
Caramelized Apple
Toss 2 cups diced peeled apples with 1 tablespoon lemon juice. Melt 2 tablespoons butter in a separate 10-inch skillet over medium-high heat; add 2 tablespoons sugar and the apples. Cook, stirring frequently, until apples are lightly caramelized, 5-7 minutes. Add 1/4 cup raisins, 1 tablespoon applejack, and 1/4 teaspoon ground cinnamon; cook 1 minute. Stir apples into batter before pouring into skillet.
Pineapple-Kirsch
Marinate 1 cup diced fresh or drained canned unsweetened pineapple in 1/4 cup kirsch for 20 minutes. Drain; add to skillet. Pour in batter. Substitute brown sugar for confectioner's sugar.
Toppings:
Rum-Glazed Banana
Cut 2 firm ripe bananas diagonally into 1-inch slices; toss with 2 tablespoons lemon juice. Melt 2 tablespoons butter in a separate 10-inch skillet over medium heat. Add bananas; sauté 30 seconds. Add 3 tablespoons dark rum, carefully ignite, and cook 1 minute. Spoon warm topping over baked Basic Pancake. Substitute brown sugar for confectioner's sugar.
Cranberry-Orange
Heat 1 1/2 cups fresh or frozen cranberries, 1/2 cup brown sugar, 3 tablespoons orange juice, 1 teaspoon grated orange zest, and 1/2 teaspoon ground cinnamon in a heavy medium saucepan over medium heat to boiling. Cook, stirring frequently, until thickened, 4-5 minutes. Remove from heat; stir in 1 1/2 teaspoons orange-flavored liqueur and small pears cut into wedges. Refrigerate for 30 minutes; serve with baked Basic Pancake.
